channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Zimbabwe lab sees regional gold hunt accelerate as prices soar
item.description: Bloomberg Gold’s 75% surge over the past two years is spurring exploration in parts of southern Africa not typically known for large-scale production, according to a Zimbabwean laboratory that has seen a jump in samples. The samples have come from Madagascar and Mozambique, said Kiran Desai, managing director of privately owned Performance Laboratories, in an [...] The post Zimbabwe lab sees regional gold hunt accelerate as prices soar appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: New coalition declares war on CAB3: ‘They are dismantling the Constitution, not amending it’ @ZANUPF_Official
item.description: A FRESH political front has opened against Constitutional Amendment Bill Number 3 (CAB3) amid the launch of a new political formation to challenge Zanu PF hegemony. Launched Friday under the banner Sungano yeVanhu/Ubumbano lomphakathi, or The People’s Coalition, the new coalition walked straight into CAB3 with its boots on. Its opening salvo came from United [...] The post New coalition declares war on CAB3: ‘They are dismantling the Constitution, not amending it’ appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: ‘They are dismantling the Constitution, not amending it’ – New coalition unites churches, labour and civic groups against CAB3 @ZANUPF_Official
item.description: A fresh political front has emerged against Constitutional Amendment Bill No. 3 (CAB3), with a newly launched coalition positioning itself as a challenger to Zanu PF’s political dominance while vowing to block the contentious legislation. Launched on Friday under the banner Sungano yeVanhu/Ubumbano lomphakathi (The People’s Coalition), the alliance wasted no time in declaring its [...] The post ‘They are dismantling the Constitution, not amending it’ – New coalition unites churches, labour and civic groups against CAB3 appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Sables suffer heartbreak in Denver despite second-half fightback @3menonaboat
item.description:
Zimbabwe suffered from a Tonga first-half onslaught

The post Sables suffer heartbreak in Denver despite second-half fightback first appeared on Three Men On a Boat.

(via Three Men On Boat) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Three Men On Boat

item.title: FULL DETAILS: Mushikashika driver arrested for thoroughly beating up police officer at Service Station (WATCH VIDEO)
item.description: The Service Station Scuffle: Behind the Mutare Mushikashika Viral Bashing MUTARE – The grainy footage begins like so many others captured on a smartphone in contemporary Zimbabwe: a flurry of motion, the distinct sound of a scuffle, and the stunned silence of onlookers. But as the camera focuses, the scene at a local service station [...] The post FULL DETAILS: Mushikashika driver arrested for thoroughly beating up police officer at Service Station (WATCH VIDEO) first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Comrade Martin Nyathi Celebrates 70 Years in Grand Style
item.description: _Family, Friends and Comrades Honour a Life of Faith and Love. By Dr Masimba Mavaza CORBY/ZIMBABWE — Veteran comrade and... Continue reading "Comrade Martin Nyathi Celebrates 70 Years in Grand Style" (via Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe News

item.title: Lobola Refund With 10% Interest per Annum: Chief Mutasa’s Ruling Sets Shocking Precedent in Traditional Justice
item.description: MUTARE – In a truly unprecedented and potentially groundbreaking ruling, Chief Mutasa has ordered a father-in-law to refund the lobola (bride price) paid for his daughter, along with a 10 per cent annual interest, after she left her husband and moved in with his nephew. This extraordinary decision, emerging from the traditional justice system, has [...] The post Lobola Refund With 10% Interest per Annum: Chief Mutasa’s Ruling Sets Shocking Precedent in Traditional Justice first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Chris Brown Ordered to Pay Nearly $13 Million in Damages Over Housekeeper’s Dog Attack Lawsuit
item.description: R&B superstar Chris Brown has been ordered to pay nearly $13 million in damages after a California jury ruled in favour of a former housekeeper who suffered severe and permanent injuries in a dog attack at the singer’s Los Angeles-area home. The verdict stems from an incident at Brown’s Tarzana, California, residence, where former employee [...] The post Chris Brown Ordered to Pay Nearly $13 Million in Damages Over Housekeeper’s Dog Attack Lawsuit appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: China’s Chery Acquires Former Nissan Plant in South Africa, in African Manufacturing Expansion
item.description: JOHANNESBURG – Chinese automotive giant Chery has officially taken control of Nissan’s former manufacturing plant in Rosslyn, South Africa, in a landmark investment that is expected to create nearly 3,000 jobs while reinforcing the country’s position as Africa’s leading automotive production hub. The acquisition marks a significant milestone in Chery’s African growth strategy as Chinese [...] The post China’s Chery Acquires Former Nissan Plant in South Africa, in African Manufacturing Expansion appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: ZRP Arrests Mutare Man Caught on Camera Assaulting a Police Officer @PoliceZimbabwe
item.description: ZRP Arrests Mutare Man Caught on Camera Assaulting Police Officer in Viral Incident The Zimbabwe Republic Police (ZRP) has arrested a 29-year-old Mutare man in connection with the assault of a police officer during an incident that was captured on video and widely shared on social media. The arrest follows the circulation of footage showing [...] The post ZRP Arrests Mutare Man Caught on Camera Assaulting a Police Officer appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

Continue reading "Mnangagwa Boasts Of Second Republic Success Despite Citizens’ Worsening Challenges" (via Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews

item.title: 50 Cent Expands Starz Television Slate With Supernatural Crime Drama Bone Parish
item.description: 50 Cent is once again strengthening his footprint in television, with Starz moving forward on a new supernatural crime drama adaptation titled Bone Parish, based on Cullen Bunn’s acclaimed graphic novel. The project marks another major collaboration between the rapper-turned-producer and the premium cable network, as he continues to expand his G-Unit Film & Television [...] The post 50 Cent Expands Starz Television Slate With Supernatural Crime Drama Bone Parish appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: Tichatonga kusvika madhonga amera nyanga: President Mnangagwa reveals @ZANUPF_Official
item.description: ZANU-PF’s Enduring Grip: Mnangagwa’s ‘Donkeys Grow Horns’ Declaration Amidst Political Tensions and Human Rights Concerns Harare – President Emmerson Mnangagwa, leader of Zimbabwe’s ruling ZANU-PF party, recently reiterated a powerful and historically charged declaration, asserting that the party’s dominance would persist until “donkeys grow horns.” This potent metaphor, originally coined by the late Vice President [...] The post Tichatonga kusvika madhonga amera nyanga: President Mnangagwa reveals first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Ipec targets pension defaulters
item.description: Zimbabwe’s pensions regulator has threatened to crack down on major State-owned enterprises and other employers that have failed to remit millions of United States dollars in pension contributions deducted from employees, warning that persistent defaulters face garnishee orders. In its first-quarter report, the Insurance and Pensions Commission (Ipec) said total outstanding pension contribution arrears rose [...] The post Ipec targets pension defaulters appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: Quiet Money, Deep Roots: How the Oak Foundation Backs Zimbabwe’s Communities
item.description: The Geneva-based Oak Foundation has funded Zimbabwean causes since 1983. In 2025 its Zimbabwe programme made USD 7.9 million in grants, backing palliative care, landmine clearance, regenerative farming and care for children who have lost parents. #Zimbabwe #Policy #ZimbabwePolicy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tapiwa Hillary Phillipo
